title: Non-tech summaries 2016: projects on endocrine system
body: "This document outlines the projects granted under the Animals (Scientific Procedures) Act 1986 during 2016 with a primary purpose of basic research, endocrine system / metabolism.  The following projects were granted:    regulation of metabolism and cognition: young to old (VGF, small rodents, memory and learning, energy balance)   the physiological roles of clathrin diversity (clathrin, biochemistry, cell biology, diabetes)   biological significance of endocrine cells in diabetes (glucose, hormone, pancreas, gut, diabetes)   the role of genes and environment in diabetes in rodents (diabetes, imaging, drugs, mouse, human)   glucose sensing mechanisms in diabetes (hypoglycaemia diabetes central nervous system)   mechanisms of adipose (fat) tissue dysfunction (adipose or fat tissue, obesity, lipodystrophy, insulin resistance, diabetes)   mechanisms of metabolic regulation in health and disease (cancer, metabolism, obesity, diet, imaging)   mechanisms of metabolic disease (obesity, diabetes, adipose, fat)   understanding energy balance in health and disease (obesity, metabolism, appetite, cachexia.)   impact of exercise and high fat/sugar diet on physical and mental health (voluntary exercise, high fat/high sugar diet, epigenetics, wellbeing, prevention)    "
